title: Browser extension (MV3): Pi in a side panel + send-page-context action
current_state: No browser presence. Ash explicitly wants a web extension as a Pi surface.
target_state: Chrome MV3 extension: side panel hosts the same chat client as T-189 (shared HTML/JS, pointed at http://127.0.0.1:7712); context-menu action 'Ask Pi about this page' sends selection/URL/title as the message prefix. Its own persistent conversation_id (extension:default). No remote calls except the local brain server; no content scripts beyond selection capture.
migration_plan: 1. extension/manifest.json: sidePanel + contextMenus + activeTab permissions, host_permission http://127.0.0.1:7712/*.
2. Reuse web/index.html chat core as the side panel (single source — factor shared JS into web/chat.js consumed by both).
3. sw.js: context-menu click → capture {selection, url, title} → prefix message → open panel.
4. Load-unpacked dev install documented in extension/README.md.
5. Tests: pure-function message-prefix builder unit-tested in repo; extension shell is manual-verified (document the checklist).
risk_notes: The extension is a THIN CLIENT — any temptation to put intelligence in it is a smell. CORS: server must allow the extension origin for 127.0.0.1 requests (chrome-extension:// origin header).
